---
title: Feature Engineering
---

`q.feature` collects feature engineering tools under one namespace, organized as
submodules:

| Submodule | What it is |
|---|---|
| `q.feature.transforms` | General-purpose feature transformations |
| `q.feature.talib` | every [TA-Lib](https://ta-lib.github.io/ta-lib-python/) indicator, pandas-friendly |
| `q.feature.pandas_ta` | every [pandas-ta-classic](https://xgboosted.github.io/pandas-ta-classic/) indicator |

All indicator functions share the same convention: they accept an **OHLCV DataFrame**
with lowercase columns (`open, high, low, close, volume`) — the frame layout returned
by `q.data.sources`'s `read()` functions (e.g. `q.data.sources.yfinance.read(...)`) — and
preserve the datetime index on the output. Indicators that only need a price series
also accept a plain `Series` (treated as `close`).

Pages:

- [Transforms](transforms.ipynb) — general-purpose feature transformations, plus
  visualizing features with `q.plot.col`.
- [TA-Lib](talib.ipynb) — the `q.feature.talib` wrapper.
- [pandas-ta-classic](pandas_ta.ipynb) — the `q.feature.pandas_ta` wrapper.

## IDE support

The `talib` and `pandas_ta` wrappers create their functions dynamically at runtime.
Autocomplete and hover docs are provided by generated type stubs — regenerate them
after upgrading either library:

```bash
make stubs
```
